response.justification: Aisha's profile is that of a strong full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with deep expertise in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the role's non-negotiable core is 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development for industrial automation platforms. C++ does not appear in her skills list and surfaces only in a 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis audio project — nowhere near the senior fluency required. She shows no exposure to IEC 62443 or SDL secure-coding practices, no industrial-protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), and no Azure work; her cloud background is AWS CDK. The advantageous Node.js and React skills are real but cannot compensate for these fundamental gaps in the role's primary requirements.
strengths: Proven event-driven and distributed-systems experience: micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic, plus an independent event-driven GIS platform
Strong software-craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a consistent record of community mentoring via workshops and code-kata sessions
Polyglot breadth with low-level systems exposure (DLang async I/O, C/C++ at NI LabWindows, robotic-arm work at Altom) alongside full-stack web skills that match several advantageous criteria
concerns: C++ is effectively absent: unlisted in the skills section, with only 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and one thesis project — far below the senior depth the role demands
No evidence of industrial-automation, OT/ICS security, or any required industrial protocol (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, OPC UA, DNP 3.0) or cybersecurity standard (IEC 62443, SDL)

Education is a BSc in Computer Science rather than the B.E./B.Tech engineering degree specified, and her entire career trajectory is web and cloud-native, not embedded or on-premises industrial systems
